message:
feat(setup): add SSH domain Lua generation and fixture-based tests

Extend setup module with SSH domain configuration generation and
improve test coverage with fixture-based testing.

New functionality:
- generate_ssh_domains_lua(): Generate WA-managed wezterm.lua block
  with ssh_domains configuration from parsed SSH hosts
- lua_escape(): Helper for escaping Lua string literals
- Improved redact_identity_path() to handle edge cases

Test improvements:
- Add setup_fixture() helper to load test fixtures by name
- Add fixture files for wezterm and shell config testing:
  - wezterm_missing.lua: Config without WA block
  - wezterm_present.lua: Config with existing WA block
  - shell_missing.bashrc: Shell config without WA block
  - shell_present.bashrc: Shell config with existing WA block
- Add test_patch_fixture_missing_inserts_once()
- Add test_patch_fixture_present_is_idempotent()
- Add test_shell_patch_fixture_missing_inserts_once()
- Add test_shell_patch_fixture_present_is_idempotent()

docs/remote-setup-spec.md: Specification for remote setup feature

Also sync beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.8","title":"[Human command] `wa workflow` (run workflow manually; show step logs/progress)","description":"# Task: Human command wa workflow

## Goal
Allow humans to manually run a workflow against a pane for debugging/recovery.

## Command examples
- wa workflow run handle_compaction <pane_id>
- wa workflow run handle_usage_limits <pane_id> --force
- wa workflow show <execution_id>

## Workspace/config
- --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
- --config (optional)

## UX
- TTY: show step-by-step progress as it runs (or tail step logs if running asynchronously).
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Safety
- Same policy gates as workflows run automatically.
- Surface policy decisions clearly.

## Testing
- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (args/exit codes/JSON stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - core workflow behaviors are validated by E2E scripts under `wa-4vx.10.*` (compaction, usage limits, resume-after-restart).

## Logging & debuggability
- The command output must always include (TTY or JSON):
  - `workflow_execution_id`
  - final outcome (success/aborted/denied) + reason
  - a short per-step summary (waits/sends) with redaction

This makes “what happened?” answerable without digging in DB tables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A human can run a workflow and see a clear, minimal summary of:
  - steps executed
  - waits/timeouts
  - sends performed (redacted)
  - final outcome

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.9","title":"[Human command] `wa audit` (recent actions feed; filter by pane/workflow; redacted)","description":"# Task: Human command wa audit

## Goal
Expose the audit trail to humans for debugging and trust.

## Features
- wa audit shows recent actions.
- Filters:
  - --pane-id
  - --actor human|robot|mcp|workflow
  - --since / --limit
- Workspace selection:
  - --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
  - --config (optional)

## Output
- TTY: rich table/panels with severity highlighting for denies/failures.
- Non-TTY: JSONL for piping.

## Safety
- Redaction is always applied.

## Reservation visibility
- Reservation conflicts/denials should be visible with owner + reason + TTL (redacted where needed).

## Testing
- Storage-level tests:
  - covered by `wa-4vx.3.9` (insert/query, redaction, retention)

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (filter flags, ordering, JSONL stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - policy denial + approval E2E scripts should assert audit rows are emitted with redaction.
  - reservation E2E (`wa-nu4.1.6.4`) should assert audit shows conflict details.

## Logging & debuggability
- `wa audit` should be able to answer “why?” without requiring verbose logs.
- When `--verbose` is enabled, include:
  - resolved workspace/DB path
  - applied filters
  - result count

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can answer: "Why didn’t wa send text?" by looking at wa audit.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3","title":"[EPIC] Setup automation: wa setup (wezterm.lua lane, shell integration, SSH hosts)","description":"# [EPIC] Setup & configuration automation

## Mission
Make wa easy to install and hard to misconfigure.

## Why
wa’s correctness depends on:
- having sufficient scrollback / capture strategy
- prompt boundary markers (OSC 133 / user-vars)
- a forwarding lane from WezTerm to wa (wezterm.lua)

Humans should not have to hand-edit large config files or remember magic snippets.

## Scope
- Local setup (`wa setup`): idempotent modifications and installs.
- SSH host discovery for future remote support.

## Non-goals (v0.1)
- Full remote orchestration across machines (Phase 5+)

## Testing & safety (non-negotiable)
Setup is where users decide whether they trust wa. The setup path must be:
- non-destructive
- idempotent
- transparent (shows diffs / planned edits)

Test coverage must include:
- fixture-based unit tests for:
  - ssh config parsing
  - idempotent patching logic (wezterm.lua + shell)

- E2E scripts with artifacts:
  - `wa setup` run twice produces the same result (idempotency)
  - logs and patched files are captured for debugging

## Definition of done
- A new machine can get to a working capture + events pipeline with a single guided `wa setup`.

## Success Criteria
- `wa setup` provides a safe, idempotent path to install wezterm.lua lane and shell integration.
- Setup scripts avoid destructive changes and include clear rollback instructions.
- E2E/manual validation steps exist with detailed logging for troubleshooting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.1","title":"SSH host discovery: parse ~/.ssh/config into structured hosts","description":"# Task: SSH host discovery (parse ~/.ssh/config)

## Goal
Parse `~/.ssh/config` into a structured list of "real" hosts (ignore wildcards) to support:
- future remote domain setup
- human UX (pick a host)

## Requirements
- Ignore wildcard hosts (`*`, `?`).
- Preserve useful fields:
  - Host alias
  - HostName
  - User
  - Port
  - IdentityFile(s)
- Be tolerant of comments/whitespace.

## Safety & privacy
- Do not attempt to read or validate private keys.
- Treat identity file paths as potentially sensitive and redact where appropriate in logs.

## Deliverables
- `SshHost` struct + parser.
- `wa setup --list-hosts` output (can be stubbed if CLI not ready).

## Testing
- Fixture-based unit tests (no real SSH required):
  - representative `~/.ssh/config` fixtures
  - comments/whitespace
  - multiple `Host` stanzas
  - ignored wildcard stanzas
- Ensure stable ordering and deterministic parsing output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative fixture file, parser returns expected hosts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","title":"E2E script: wa setup idempotent patching (wezterm.lua + shell) with artifacts","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa setup idempotency

## Goal
Validate that wa setup is truly safe and idempotent.

Setup commands are uniquely user-sensitive: a bug can corrupt dotfiles and destroy trust.
This E2E test provides a safety net with great logging and artifacts.

## Scenario
Run against a temporary fake "home" directory (never touch real dotfiles):
- Provide fixture files:
  - wezterm.lua (various shapes)
  - .zshrc / .bashrc / config.fish fixtures
  - optional ~/.ssh/config fixture

Steps
1) wa setup --dry-run
   - asserts: prints what it would change without writing

2) wa setup (apply mode)
   - asserts: writes only within the temp home

3) wa setup again
   - asserts: no further changes (idempotent)

## Assertions
- wezterm.lua contains exactly one wa-managed block (WA-BEGIN/WA-END) after apply.
- Running again makes zero changes.
- Shell integration is installed once and is idempotent.
- Output is verbose, timestamped, and never contains secrets.

## Artifacts
- before/after copies of each modified file
- unified diff outputs
- full command logs

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic and safe to run in CI.
- Failures clearly identify which patcher step is not idempotent.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add an explicit guard that the test never writes outside the temp home (assert all modified paths are under the temp root).
  - Include at least one “hostile fixture” variant (wezterm.lua already contains a partial WA block / malformed markers) to ensure patcher behavior is safe.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.11","title":"E2E harness: wa setup remote against dockerized sshd (artifacts, rollback safety)","description":"# Task: E2E harness — wa setup remote

## Goal
Provide a repeatable E2E test for wa setup remote without requiring real servers.

Remote setup is high-risk (it touches:
- remote files
- systemd user services
- installs binaries/configs)

An E2E harness helps us ship confidently.

## Approach
- Use a dockerized sshd container as the "remote host".
- Run wa setup remote against it with:
  - dry-run mode
  - apply mode
  - idempotency verification (run twice)

## Safety constraints
- The harness must guarantee it only touches the container filesystem.
- Always run with verbose logging and produce artifacts.

## Assertions
- Remote install steps are idempotent.
- systemd user service unit file is created/updated as expected.
- Logs include clear step start/end markers and show any rollback path taken.

## Artifacts
- captured stdout/stderr
- remote filesystem snapshot (or tarball of touched paths)
- systemd unit contents

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The harness runs locally and in CI (where supported) without flakes.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the harness refuses to run if the “remote host” is not clearly the docker container (avoid accidental real-host modification).
  - Add a failure-injection step (e.g., simulate missing package manager) and ensure rollback reporting is captured in artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","title":"Decide default scrollback_lines + setup/doctor guidance","description":"# Task: Decide default scrollback_lines + setup/doctor guidance

## Goal
Resolve the open question: what scrollback depth gives reliable capture without excessive memory usage.

## Why
wa depends on scrollback for capture fidelity. Too low → gaps and missed detections; too high → memory bloat.
We need a documented default and clear warnings.

## Work items
- Benchmark memory/CPU impact for a range (e.g., 10k / 50k / 100k lines) on typical machines.
- Choose a default (tentative: 50k) and document the tradeoffs.
- Update config defaults and `wa setup` guidance.
- Update `wa doctor` to warn when scrollback is below the recommended minimum.

## Deliverables
- Decision note in this bead (final default + rationale).
- Config default in `wa.toml` schema.
- Setup/doctor output reflects the chosen recommendation.

## Testing
- Unit tests for the doctor warning threshold.
- Setup dry-run output includes the recommended value.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scrollback_lines is chosen and documented.
- Users get actionable warnings when their config is below the minimum.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.2","title":"Idempotent wezterm.lua patch: user-var forwarding lane to wa","description":"# Task: Idempotent `wezterm.lua` patch (user-var forwarding lane)

## Goal
Automate installation of a small `wezterm.lua` snippet that forwards wa-related `user-var-changed` events to wa.

## Why
- OSC 133 markers are good, but user-vars give us an extra deterministic channel.
- Forwarding is necessary for future features (explicit checkpoints, prompt boundaries, "agent ready" signals).
- Humans should not need to copy/paste Lua.

## Minimal forwarding lane (PLAN Appendix E.1)
This is the exact minimal Lua we must be able to install idempotently:

```lua
-- Forward user-var events to wa daemon
wezterm.on('user-var-changed', function(window, pane, name, value)
  if name:match('^wa%-') then
    wezterm.background_child_process {
      'wa', 'event', '--from-uservar',
      '--pane', tostring(pane:pane_id()),
      '--name', name,
      '--value', value
    }
  end
end)
```

Notes:
- Prefix filter: default is `wa-` (hyphen). If we later support `wa_`, the filter must remain strict and configurable.
- The background child process must be non-blocking for WezTerm UI responsiveness.

## Approach
- `wa setup` locates the active `wezterm.lua`.
- It applies an idempotent patch:
  - insert snippet if missing
  - do nothing if already present
- It creates a backup copy (non-destructive).

## Forwarding lane contract
- We only forward vars matching the configured prefix.
- The Lua code calls:
  - `wa event --from-uservar --pane <id> --name <name> --value <value>`
- wa decodes base64 payloads where applicable and emits an internal signal/event.

## Deliverables
- Lua snippet template (managed block with WA-BEGIN/WA-END markers).
- WezTerm config locator.
- Idempotent patcher.

## Testing
- Unit/fixture tests (see `wa-nu4.3.3.5`):
  - patcher inserts exactly one managed block
  - re-running is a no-op
  - backups are created and paths are reported
- E2E:
  - `wa-nu4.3.3.10` validates end-to-end idempotency in a temp HOME
  - `wa-4vx.10.14` validates user-var events flow through the forwarding lane

## Acceptance Criteria
- Re-running `wa setup` does not duplicate the snippet.
- Manual review of the file shows only the intended additions.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.3","title":"Shell integration installer: enable OSC 133 prompt markers (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"# Task: Shell integration installer (OSC 133 markers)

## Goal
Provide a guided, idempotent way to enable OSC 133 prompt markers so wa can detect:
- prompt boundaries
- command start/end
- "pane is idle/ready" reliably

## Why
We want to avoid brittle heuristics that guess prompts based on `❯`/`$`.
OSC 133 is the robust path.

## Minimal marker emission (PLAN §4.6; illustrative)
The installer should ultimately produce equivalent semantics to:

```bash
# ~/.config/wa/shell-integration.bash (sourced by user's .bashrc)
__wa_prompt_start() { printf '\e]133;A\a'; }
__wa_command_start() { printf '\e]133;C\a'; }
__wa_command_end() { printf '\e]133;D;%s\a' "$?"; }
PROMPT_COMMAND='__wa_prompt_start'
trap '__wa_command_end' DEBUG  # Simplified; real impl must be more robust
```

Notes:
- This snippet is intentionally simplified in the plan; the real implementation must avoid breaking shells.
- The key requirement is emitting the correct OSC 133 sequences so wa can deterministically infer PromptActive/CommandRunning and exit codes.

## Approach
- `wa setup --shell` detects current shell (and can accept `--shell bash|zsh|fish`).
- Install minimal shell snippets into appropriate rc files:
  - `~/.bashrc`, `~/.zshrc`, `~/.config/fish/config.fish`
- Changes are idempotent and include a clear managed marker block.
- Always create a backup copy before modifying.

## Deliverables
- Shell snippet templates.
- Detection and idempotent patching logic.

## Testing
- Unit/fixture tests (see `wa-nu4.3.3.5`):
  - inserts exactly one managed block
  - re-running is a no-op
  - backups are created
- E2E:
  - `wa-nu4.3.3.10` validates idempotent patching in a temp HOME with artifacts

## Acceptance Criteria
- After setup, a sample interactive session emits OSC 133 markers.
- wa ingest sees those markers and updates deterministic state.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.6","title":"Remote setup spec: wezterm install, mux server service, wa install, safety/rollback","description":"# Task: Remote setup spec (`wa setup remote`)

## Goal
Define a robust, user-friendly remote bootstrap flow that makes WezTerm domains reliable:
- install WezTerm if missing
- run `wezterm-mux-server` as a user systemd service
- enable linger so the mux survives logout
- optionally install `wa` on the remote

## Why
A core user journey in the plan is:
> “Bring a new remote host online: install matching WezTerm, set up mux server, standardize config.”

## Safety requirements
- Must be **explicit** and non-surprising:
  - default to `--dry-run`
  - require confirmation for any operation that changes the remote host
- Must be **idempotent**:
  - re-run safely
  - detect existing installs/services
- Must be **observable**:
  - verbose logging and step timing
  - clear final summary with next steps

## Plan (proposed)
1. Choose host (from SSH config or explicit `--host`).
2. Verify connectivity.
3. Detect WezTerm presence + version.
4. If missing:
   - detect package manager (apt/dnf)
   - install WezTerm (documented script)
5. Install systemd user service unit for `wezterm-mux-server`.
6. `systemctl --user enable --now wezterm-mux-server`.
7. `loginctl enable-linger $USER` (requires sudo; explain).
8. Verify service is active.
9. Optionally scp `wa` binary to `~/.local/bin/wa`.

## Deliverables
- A written contract for:
  - CLI flags
  - what remote commands we run
  - what files we create
  - what errors we surface

## Testing
- Fixture-based tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.3.9` (dry-run plan output stability, command rendering, logging)

- E2E coverage:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.3.11` (dockerized sshd harness + artifacts + rollback safety)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reviewer can implement remote setup from this issue without re-reading `PLAN.md`.
- The spec includes a rollback story (how to disable service / undo).

## Goal
Implement the remote setup subcommand described in `wa-nu4.3.3.6`.

## Requirements
- Must support `--dry-run` and `--yes`/confirmation.
- Must emit detailed logs (including per-step durations).

  - handles already-installed wezterm
  - handles existing systemd service

- Remote command runner wrappers (`ssh`, `scp`) with:
  - timeouts
  - stdout/stderr capture
  - redaction for any accidental secrets
- Remote install flows:
  - apt-based
  - dnf-based
- Service unit installation using an embedded template.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running against a test host:
  - installs/ensures wezterm
  - starts mux service
  - verifies active
  - prints a clear summary with follow-up steps


## Testing
- E2E tests:
  - Use the dockerized sshd harness (`wa-nu4.3.3.11`) to validate:
    - dry-run does not modify the host
    - apply mode installs/starts service
    - idempotent second run makes no further changes
    - failures produce artifact bundles (remote stdout/stderr, transcripts)

- Safety tests:
  - Add a “rollback safety” scenario: if a step fails mid-way, ensure the command reports what may have changed and what to do next.

+{"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.6","title":"Remote setup spec: wezterm install, mux server service, wa install, safety/rollback","description":"# Task: Remote setup spec (`wa setup remote`)

## Goal
Define a robust, user-friendly remote bootstrap flow that makes WezTerm domains reliable:
- install WezTerm if missing
- run `wezterm-mux-server` as a user systemd service
- enable linger so the mux survives logout
- optionally install `wa` on the remote

## Why
A core user journey in the plan is:
> “Bring a new remote host online: install matching WezTerm, set up mux server, standardize config.”

## Safety requirements
- Must be **explicit** and non-surprising:
  - default to `--dry-run`
  - require confirmation for any operation that changes the remote host
- Must be **idempotent**:
  - re-run safely
  - detect existing installs/services
- Must be **observable**:
  - verbose logging and step timing
  - clear final summary with next steps

## Plan (proposed)
1. Choose host (from SSH config or explicit `--host`).
2. Verify connectivity.
3. Detect WezTerm presence + version.
4. If missing:
   - detect package manager (apt/dnf)
   - install WezTerm (documented script)
5. Install systemd user service unit for `wezterm-mux-server`.
6. `systemctl --user enable --now wezterm-mux-server`.
7. `loginctl enable-linger $USER` (requires sudo; explain).
8. Verify service is active.
9. Optionally scp `wa` binary to `~/.local/bin/wa`.

## Deliverables
- A written contract for:
  - CLI flags
  - what remote commands we run
  - what files we create
  - what errors we surface

## Testing
- Fixture-based tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.3.9` (dry-run plan output stability, command rendering, logging)

- E2E coverage:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.3.11` (dockerized sshd harness + artifacts + rollback safety)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reviewer can implement remote setup from this issue without re-reading `PLAN.md`.
- The spec includes a rollback story (how to disable service / undo).

+local wezterm = require 'wezterm'
+local config = {}
+
+-- WA-BEGIN (do not edit this block)
+-- Forward user-var events to wa daemon
+wezterm.on('user-var-changed', function(window, pane, name, value)
+  if name:match('^wa%-') then
+    wezterm.background_child_process {
+      'wa', 'event', '--from-uservar',
+      '--pane', tostring(pane:pane_id()),
+      '--name', name,
+      '--value', value
+    }
+  end
+end)
+-- WA-END

+# Remote Setup Spec (wa setup remote)
+
+## Summary
+A guided, idempotent, non-destructive workflow to bootstrap a remote host for WezTerm mux usage:
+- verify SSH connectivity
+- detect OS + package manager
+- install WezTerm if missing
+- install and enable `wezterm-mux-server` as a systemd user service
+- enable linger so the mux survives logout
+- optionally install `wa` on the remote
+
+Default behavior is dry-run with a full plan preview. No destructive actions are allowed.
+
+---
+
+## Goals
+- Make remote WezTerm domains reliable and repeatable.
+- Provide a single command to bootstrap a host safely.
+- Keep output clear, auditable, and deterministic.
+
+## Non-Goals
+- Managing SSH keys (assumes SSH access already works).
+- Managing sudo credentials beyond explicit prompts.
+
+---
+
+## CLI Contract
+
+### Command
+```
+wa setup remote --host <ssh_host>
+```
+
+### Flags
+- `--host <ssh_host>`: SSH alias from `~/.ssh/config` or explicit host.
+- `--dry-run`: default; prints plan, does not modify remote.
+- `--apply`: executes the plan (non-destructive). Requires explicit confirmation.
+- `--install-wa`: include installing `wa` on the remote.
+- `--wa-path <path>`: optional local path to `wa` binary for scp.
+- `--wa-version <version|git>`: if not using scp, specify install source.
+- `--yes`: skip interactive prompts (only allowed with `--apply`).
+- `--timeout-secs <n>`: per-command timeout (default 30s).
+- `--verbose`: emit step-by-step logs with timings and remote command outputs.
+
+### Output Format
+- Human output by default.
+- If `WA_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json`, also emit machine-parsable JSON plan/results.
+
+---
+
+## Safety Requirements
+- Default to `--dry-run`.
+- Explicit confirmation before any remote change.
+- All file mutations create backups or are additive.
+- No deletion of remote user data.
+
+---
+
+## Step Plan (Dry Run + Apply)
+
+### 1) Host Selection
+- Accept `--host` or prompt from SSH config (if interactive).
+- Resolve to `ssh` target and report effective user/hostname/port.
+
+### 2) Connectivity Check
+Run:
+```
+ssh <host> "true"
+```
+- If unreachable, abort with actionable error.
+
+### 3) Detect OS / Package Manager
+Run:
+```
+ssh <host> "command -v apt-get || command -v dnf || command -v yum || command -v pacman || true"
+```
+- Record package manager for later steps.
+
+### 4) Detect WezTerm
+Run:
+```
+ssh <host> "command -v wezterm"
+ssh <host> "wezterm --version"    # if wezterm exists
+```
+- If missing and `--apply`, proceed to install.
+
+### 5) Install WezTerm (If Missing)
+Plan depends on package manager:
+
+#### apt (Ubuntu/Debian)
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo apt-get update"
+ssh <host> "sudo apt-get install -y wezterm"
+```
+
+#### dnf (Fedora)
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo dnf install -y wezterm"
+```
+
+#### yum (RHEL/CentOS)
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo yum install -y wezterm"
+```
+
+#### pacman (Arch)
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo pacman -Sy --noconfirm wezterm"
+```
+
+If no known manager:
+- Stop and report unsupported OS. Provide guidance for manual install.
+
+### 6) Install systemd user service for mux
+Service file path:
+```
+~/.config/systemd/user/wezterm-mux-server.service
+```
+Service content (template):
+```
+[Unit]
+Description=WezTerm Mux Server
+After=network.target
+
+[Service]
+Type=simple
+ExecStart=/usr/bin/wezterm-mux-server --daemonize=false
+Restart=on-failure
+RestartSec=2
+
+[Install]
+WantedBy=default.target
+```
+
+Commands:
+```
+ssh <host> "mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user"
+ssh <host> "cat > ~/.config/systemd/user/wezterm-mux-server.service <<'EOF'\n...EOF"
+ssh <host> "systemctl --user daemon-reload"
+ssh <host> "systemctl --user enable --now wezterm-mux-server"
+```
+
+### 7) Enable linger (mux survives logout)
+Command (requires sudo):
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo loginctl enable-linger $USER"
+```
+- If sudo denied, print remediation steps; do not retry silently.
+
+### 8) Verify service
+```
+ssh <host> "systemctl --user status wezterm-mux-server"
+```
+- Parse status; report active/inactive.
+
+### 9) Optional: Install wa on remote
+If `--install-wa`:
+- Preferred: scp local binary
+```
+scp <local_wa> <host>:~/.local/bin/wa
+ssh <host> "chmod +x ~/.local/bin/wa"
+```
+- Alternative (if no local binary):
+```
+ssh <host> "cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/wezterm_automata.git wa"
+```
+
+---
+
+## Idempotency Rules
+- If WezTerm already installed, skip install.
+- If service file exists and matches expected content, skip rewrite.
+- If service is enabled/active, skip enable step.
+- If linger already enabled, skip.
+- If `wa` binary already present and matches version (if detectable), skip.
+
+---
+
+## Observability
+- Each step logs:
+  - command string (redacted where needed)
+  - duration
+  - stdout/stderr (redacted)
+  - status (ok/warn/error)
+- Final summary includes:
+  - what changed
+  - backups created
+  - next steps
+
+---
+
+## Rollback Plan
+- Disable service:
+```
+ssh <host> "systemctl --user disable --now wezterm-mux-server"
+```
+- Remove service file (manual; not automated by wa):
+```
+ssh <host> "rm ~/.config/systemd/user/wezterm-mux-server.service"
+```
+- Disable linger:
+```
+ssh <host> "sudo loginctl disable-linger $USER"
+```
+- Remove `wa` binary (manual):
+```
+ssh <host> "rm ~/.local/bin/wa"
+```
+
+---
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- A reviewer can implement remote setup without re-reading PLAN.md.
+- The spec enumerates commands, files, flags, logging, and rollback steps.
+- The flow is idempotent and safe by default.
